episodes.title: The Cult Secret That Finally Collapsed | The Hidden Third Clips w/ Mariana van Zeller
episodes.description:
Lola Blanc reveals the devastating moment her mother finally discovered the truth behind the man who claimed to be a prophet. What began as spiritual guidance slowly transformed into emotional control, manipulation, and isolation — trapping an entire family inside a reality built on fear and deception.

As the lies deepened, Lola’s mother was pushed further from her children and forced to endure humiliation in the name of faith. Letters begging for honesty went unanswered. Warnings were ignored. And while those around her enabled the manipulation, one unexpected encounter would eventually expose the horrifying truth behind it all.

This clip explores:
• How coercion can hide behind belief and authority
• The psychological power of fear, guilt, and isolation
• The moment blind faith turned into emotional captivity
• The devastating realization that everything was built on a lie

A chilling story about trust, vulnerability, and the unimaginable cost of surrendering your life to someone who never cared about the damage they caused.

🎙 From The Hidden Third with Mariana van Zeller
🎙 Guest: Lola Blanc
subjects: The Church Of Jesus Christ Of Latter-Day Saints

episodes.title: Prophet or Predator: How He Tricked Her and Trafficked Her Mother | The Hidden Third
episodes.description:
Lola Blanc was 12 years old when a man at an LDS (Mormon) singles dance told her mother he was a prophet receiving direct revelation from God. What followed wasn't a compound, a commune, or a mass following. It was something smaller, more intimate — and in many ways, more terrifying.
A one-on-one system of control that separated Lola from her mom, and escalated the situation into coercion and trafficking, all framed as God's will.
Lola's mother, Christine Marie, just starred in Trust Me: The False Prophet on Netflix — but this story is different. This one never made the headlines. The man was never fully stopped.
Today, Lola is a cult survivor, musician, filmmaker, actor (American Horror Story, Under the Silver Lake), and co-host of the Trust Me podcast, where she and her co-host Meagan Elizabeth interview cult survivors and dismantle the mechanics of coercion. Lola knows exactly how belief systems get weaponized — because it happened to her and her mother.
In this episode, she tells me the full story.

episodes.title: The Secret That Was Never Supposed to Be Heard… | The Hidden Third Clips w/ Mariana van Zeller
episodes.description:
Meagan Grainger, a former cult member, sits at the center of a conversation that began with statistics—but quickly unraveled into something far more disturbing. As Mariana van Zeller reads off the scale of cult influence in America, Meagan reveals how easily hidden systems of belief can exist in plain sight.

What begins as a discussion about numbers, influence, and recruitment turns into a deeper look inside a tightly controlled religious community—one where money, loyalty, and belief are deeply intertwined. As the conversation unfolds, revelations emerge about secrecy, power structures, and allegations that shook the foundation of the group.

This clip explores:
• How large and widespread cult-like systems may actually be
• The psychology of belief, loyalty, and information control
• How secrecy and hierarchy protect abuse and power structures
• The moment hidden truths begin to surface and spread

A story about how belief systems built on certainty can quietly shield the darkest realities—and what happens when that illusion finally breaks.

🎙 From The Hidden Third with Mariana van Zeller
🎙 Guest: Meagan Grainger

episodes.title: Everything Is a Cult… And It’s Worse Than You Think | The Hidden Third Clips w/ Mariana van Zeller
episodes.description:
Meagan Grainger, a former cult member, confronts a reality most people don’t want to see: cults aren’t rare—and they don’t always look extreme. In a conversation sparked by shocking statistics, she reveals how deeply these systems are woven into everyday life.

What begins as a discussion about millions of Americans involved in cults quickly expands into something more unsettling. From wellness groups to online communities, even spaces meant for connection can slowly introduce control—shaping behavior, thoughts, and identity without people ever realizing what they’ve stepped into.

This clip explores:
• Why cults are far more common than people believe
• How ordinary environments can become systems of control
• The psychological mechanics behind influence and belonging
• The lasting trauma and hidden consequences of these experiences

A story that challenges a simple assumption—because if everything can become a cult, the real question is… how would you know you’re in one?

🎙 From The Hidden Third with Mariana van Zeller
🎙 Guest: Meagan Grainger

episodes.title: The Christian Cult Hiding 900 Abusers | The Hidden Third with Mariana van Zeller
episodes.description:
Meagan Elizabeth was born into one of the most secretive cults in the world. Fourth generation. She didn't choose it — it was chosen for her before she could walk.

The group is known as the Two-by-Twos. Members call it The Truth. No name. No building. No written doctrine. Just families meeting in homes across 30 countries, convinced they were the only people on earth going to heaven. For over a century the group operated in near-total secrecy — sheltering at least 900 documented sexual abusers. In 2024, the FBI opened a global investigation.

In this conversation with Mariana, Meagan talks about what life inside the Two-by-Twos really looked like, how she got out, and what it takes to rebuild your identity after leaving the only world you've ever known. She's a cult survivor, comedian, and co-host of Trust Me, a podcast about cults and extreme belief, and she held nothing back.

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS 00:00 – Introduction 00:00

episodes.title: Jeffrey Epstein Survivor
episodes.description:
At 21, Lisa Phillips was assaulted by Jeffrey Epstein, an ordeal that marked the start of years of grooming and manipulation. In this conversation with Mariana, she opens up about the trauma she endured, the system that enabled it, and her ongoing fight for justice.
